other_observers: I let Richard Stevens know that I had refound the bird and he was with Allyson Marceau.; Mark Miller found the bird on March 21.
specific_location: Jumbo Reservoir - Most of the time on the sedgwick side while I was watching it. It was mostly on the east side of the county line dam barely off shore.
description: Gull just a little bit smaller than a Ring-billed Gull. Bill was dark to black, but it had some yellow on it also, mostly towards the base of the bill. Also was fairly long and thin. Black legs. Dark eye. Black ear spot on a white head and it had a bold black stripe on the back of the neck. It had a black "M" shape on the leading edge of the wings, then at the joint it shot back towards the back of the wings. Pale inner primaries and secondaries with a gray mantle. Tail had a white tail with a bold black tip. Underside of the wings were entirely white with the exception that the tip of the wings had a small amount of black. The rest of the underside of the bird was white.Behaviors: The bird was flying through the strong wind. I first saw it flying from the west towards the east just off shore, it then turned back and disappeared towards the west. A few minutes later it returned from the west. It then stayed on the east edge of the county line dam for several minutes. It then came out to the tip of the land that I was on. It just flew around in that area for about 20 minutes, sometimes disappearing below the little cliff that was below me, but then returning to view after seconds of being out of view.Call: Didn't hear itPlumage: 1st Winter
photographer: Photographer: Cole Wild, Cole Wild, Cole Wild
